choiceresponse.label: You notice that several features (e.g., ca_0, cp_2, ca_2) have large absolute coefficients. Which statements are true? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: These features strongly influence the model’s predictions across the dataset.
A patient with a positive high-magnitude feature is guaranteed to have heart disease.
Large coefficients indicate that small changes in these indicators can noticeably affect predictions.
The model may still predict low risk for a patient with a large positive coefficient if other features counteract it.
Coefficient magnitude directly measures causal importance.

div.p: Large absolute coefficients indicate that the model relies heavily on those features when forming predictions. However, predictions depend on the sum of all active contributions, so strong positive effects can be offset by negative ones. Logistic regression coefficients do not imply certainty or causality.
